$comment: Negative-case manifest for local laboratory and terminology applicability. Each case is expressed as a MUTATION of the SYNTHETIC base fixture rather than as a full duplicate document. This is deliberate: the thing under test is the difference, and a reviewer auditing 'what makes this profile expired?' should see one line, not a 130-line near-copy they must diff. It also single-sources the base document, so a schema change cannot silently leave a dozen stale fixtures that still pass. `expectedBlockers` are the codes that MUST be present; a case may legitimately raise additional blockers, but never fewer, and never zero. NOTE ON MUTATION CHOICE: a mutation must exercise the encoding that actually reaches production. The P3-V1 review found a case that used `{"op":"unset"}` where real payloads carry an explicit `null`, and the code under test only checked `=== undefined` — so the test passed while the defect was live. Prefer the value a wire format would send.

cases.failureMode: missing interval
cases.rationale: An analyte the profile does not cover must surface as missing, never fall back to a published or default interval.

cases.failureMode: conflicting intervals

analyteCode.display: Synthetic analyte A (not a real laboratory analyte)

cases.rationale: Two asserted intervals that disagree must not be resolved by picking one, ordering, or averaging.

cases.failureMode: age-band mismatch (interval's own band does not cover the request age)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F2). `intervals[].ageBand` was required by the schema and read by nothing: only the profile-level applicability band was matched. A 0-to-14-day interval could therefore be delivered for a twelve-month-old with zero blockers. Each interval's own band is now matched independently.

cases.failureMode: interval with no bounds
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F4). An interval with null bounds classified nothing and raised nothing; it was `applicable`.

cases.failureMode: interval with inverted bounds
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F4). `{low: 12, high: 2}` is empty, not permissive, and was previously accepted.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (interval sex null read as `any`)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F3). `interval.sex === null` was treated as `any`. `any` must be a deliberate assertion that the interval does not vary by sex in that band; null is an omission and fails closed.

cases.failureMode: expired profile
cases.rationale: An expired profile is not a slightly-out-of-date profile; it has no current authority.

cases.failureMode: expired profile (effective period elapsed while still marked active)
cases.rationale: The effective window is checked independently of the review state, so a stale `active` flag cannot keep an elapsed profile alive.

cases.failureMode: superseded without successor
cases.rationale: A dangling authority chain is a hard failure: the replacement interval cannot be located, so neither document can be trusted.

cases.failureMode: stale profile
cases.rationale: The profile is still marked `active` and has no effectiveEnd. Staleness must be caught on review cadence alone, or an unreviewed profile lives forever.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (review cadence)
cases.rationale: An unknown review cadence must not be read as 'no review needed'. Staleness cannot be established, so the profile fails closed.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (unparseable evaluation time silently disables expiry, effective window, and staleness)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F9). An unparseable `options.now` became NaN, and every NaN comparison is false, so expiry, not-yet-effective AND staleness were all disabled at once by a single bad input. An unparseable value must raise a blocker, never remove three.

cases.failureMode: unit mismatch (request unit differs from the matched analyte's unit)
cases.rationale: Units are compared as codes and never converted; a silent conversion at an anemia cutoff is a factor-of-ten error. The comparison is now against the requested ANALYTE's unit, not a single profile-level unit.

cases.failureMode: unit mismatch (intervals for one analyte disagree on their unit)
cases.rationale: Internal unit inconsistency inside one analyte's intervals is caught even when the inconsistent band is not the band the request selects.

cases.failureMode: analyzer mismatch
cases.rationale: A value that is normal on one analyzer can be flagged on another; intervals do not transfer across analyzers.

cases.failureMode: method mismatch
cases.rationale: Method is checked independently of analyzer: the same analyzer running a different method yields different intervals.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (analyzer method bypasses the assertion choke point)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F3). `method` was read straight off the container instead of through `readAsserted`, so a null method was COMPARED as a value rather than refused as an unasserted dimension. It happened to raise METHOD_MISMATCH, which is the wrong diagnosis — nothing was mismatched, the dimension was never asserted — and it would have been silent had the request also omitted the method.

cases.failureMode: population mismatch
cases.rationale: Intervals derived in one population are not transferable to another.

cases.failureMode: age-band mismatch
cases.rationale: An age outside the asserted band is a mismatch, never an extrapolation to the nearest band.

cases.failureMode: unbounded age band matches every child
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C3/F3). `high: null` became Infinity, so `{unit: years, low: 0, high: null}` was valid and matched every child — one band spanning birth, two weeks, and the physiologic nadir at roughly 6-9 weeks, the steepest part of the pediatric hemoglobin curve.

cases.failureMode: age bands leave a gap in the applicability range
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C3). Nothing required a profile's bands to partition the age axis, so an age inside the profile's own applicability range could have no interval at all.

cases.failureMode: age bands overlap
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C3). An overlapping band means an age with two candidate intervals and no rule for choosing between them.

cases.failureMode: age band exceeds the site's asserted maximum width
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C3). Band width was unconstrained. The acceptable width is an owner-held clinical input; the mechanism enforcing whatever the site asserts is not.

cases.failureMode: age band straddles a declared physiologic boundary

value.rationale: SYNTHETIC placeholder boundary used only to exercise the straddle check.

cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C3). A single interval cannot be correct on both sides of an age where the underlying physiology changes.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (age-band policy unresolvable)
cases.rationale: An unresolvable boundary must not be skipped. This case also proves the evaluator does not assume schema validation has already run: it fails closed on a document the schema would separately reject.

cases.failureMode: altitude mismatch
cases.rationale: Hemoglobin reference intervals shift with altitude, so altitude is a matched dimension rather than a footnote.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (null altitude bounds match every altitude)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F3). `?? -Infinity` / `?? Infinity` made a null-bounded altitude match everything, reintroducing 'sea level is assumed' by another route — the exact inference the surrounding comment says is never made.

cases.failureMode: corrected age required but not supplied
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C2). There was no representation for gestational age, postmenstrual age, prematurity, or birth weight at all, so a four-week-old born at 27 weeks and one born at 40 weeks were the same patient to this system. Anemia of prematurity has a different nadir, depth, and timing.

cases.failureMode: gestational age outside the profile's asserted applicability range
cases.rationale: A profile applies over a stated range of gestational ages at birth; outside it, its intervals were never validated.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (numeric request field supplied as a string)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F9). `"12" * 30.4375` coerces silently and the band appeared to match. A string is refused, never parsed.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (non-numeric altitude yields NaN comparisons that both pass)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F9). `NaN < low` and `NaN > high` are both false, so an unparseable altitude passed the range check silently.

cases.failureMode: missing critical value
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C11). `criticalValues[]` was required by the schema and referenced by NOTHING in either implementation. The panic threshold was modelled and never consulted, and no blocker code existed for a missing, unmapped, conflicting, or stale one. An urgent alert must dominate routine interval logic.

cases.failureMode: critical value in a different unit from its own reference interval
cases.rationale: A panic threshold compared in the wrong unit is a factor-of-ten error on the most urgent path in the system.

cases.failureMode: conflicting critical values

analyteCode.display: Synthetic analyte A (not a real laboratory analyte)

cases.rationale: Two disagreeing panic thresholds for the same analyte and age are never resolved by picking one.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (critical value not asserted)
cases.rationale: An unasserted panic threshold is not a threshold. It is refused rather than applied on the strength of its numbers alone.

cases.failureMode: critical value with neither a low nor a high threshold
cases.rationale: A critical value that can never fire is worse than an absent one: it is indistinguishable from a configured alert.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (dimension explicitly unknown)
cases.rationale: An explicitly unknown analyzer must not be treated as 'matches anything'. Unknown is representable and fails closed.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (reserved token smuggled into an asserted field)
cases.rationale: Marking a dimension `asserted` while its value is the reserved unknown token must not launder the unknown into a usable value.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (request side omits a dimension)
cases.rationale: Request-side unknowns are refused with the same rigour as profile-side unknowns; an absent analyzer is not a wildcard.

cases.failureMode: profile bound to a different candidate version
cases.rationale: Applicability is asserted against an exact candidate digest; it does not carry over to a different candidate.

cases.failureMode: intervals transferred from another source without local verification
cases.rationale: Transferred-but-unverified intervals stay representable so they remain visible, and they fail closed.

cases.failureMode: absent profile section crashes instead of failing closed
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F9). An absent `lifecycle` threw a TypeError. A crash is not a fail-closed decision — a caller may catch it and read the absence of a decision as the absence of objections.

cases.failureMode: unmapped local code
cases.rationale: A local code with no mapping is not interpretable. It must not pass through as if it were already a standard code.

cases.failureMode: conflicting local mappings
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F5). `.find` resolved a mapping conflict by first-match: two mappings for the same local code with different standard codes, units, or equivalences silently produced whichever was declared first. `INTERVAL_CONFLICT` existed for the interval side; there was no equivalent for mappings.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (null mapping unit read as a wildcard)
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F3). Unit and specimen were truthiness-guarded on BOTH sides, so a null mapping unit compared against nothing and passed, whatever unit the observation reported.

cases.failureMode: preliminary result treated as final
cases.rationale: A preliminary result is not a final result. It may be revised, so it cannot inform a decision. NOTE: this expectation changed from the overloaded RESULT_STATUS_BLOCKED, which covered five situations demanding three different human actions.

cases.failureMode: retracted result treated as merely non-decision-grade
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F7). `entered-in-error` failed closed only as 'not decision-grade'. It is a RETRACTION: the value must not be displayed or retained for interpretation at all, which is a materially different obligation and could not be expressed through a shared blocker code.

cases.failureMode: cancelled result conflated with a blocked one
cases.rationale: The test was never performed. The correct human action is to re-order, not to wait and not to discard a displayed value.

cases.failureMode: not-yet-resulted observation conflated with a blocked one
cases.rationale: No result has been reported yet. The correct human action is to wait — the opposite of re-ordering.

cases.failureMode: corrected state dropped in transit
cases.rationale: The most dangerous case: the value on hand reads `final` and authoritative while a correction exists upstream. Detected as a hard failure, not a warning.

cases.failureMode: amended state dropped in transit
cases.rationale: Amendment is treated with the same severity as correction; both supersede the value in hand.

cases.failureMode: corrected/amended state present but the superseding observation reference is explicitly null
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F6). The check tested `=== undefined`, so an explicit `null` passed — and `null` is exactly what the base observation carried and what a wire format sends. The prior test for this failure mode used `{op: unset}`, the one encoding that DID trip the check, so the suite proved the opposite of what it claimed. Knowing a correction happened is not enough; if the superseding result cannot be reached, the state is ambiguous.

cases.failureMode: corrected/amended state present but the superseding observation reference is absent
cases.rationale: The absent encoding must fail identically to the explicit-null encoding. Both are unresolvable; neither means 'no correction'.

cases.failureMode: revision that is itself superseded
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F6). The old check had the semantics INVERTED: it demanded a superseding observation from any result whose lineage recorded a revision, including a `corrected` result that IS the latest version and by definition has none. The requirement belongs on the result that is not the revision; for a result that IS the revision, a resolvable reference means something newer supersedes IT.

cases.failureMode: superseding observation reference points at the observation itself
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F6). Nothing checked for self-reference. A pointer to itself resolves nothing and hides whatever actually superseded it, while satisfying a presence check.

cases.failureMode: current status absent from the lineage that claims to describe it
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F6). Nothing checked that `status` appeared in `lineage.states`, so a lineage could describe a different observation entirely and still be accepted as proof about this one.

cases.failureMode: status lineage is not ordered
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F6). Nothing checked that the states were ordered, yet establishing which state is current is the only thing the lineage exists to prove.

cases.failureMode: corrected/amended state droppable (lineage not provable)
cases.rationale: Silence is not proof that nothing was corrected. Positive proof of a complete lineage is required.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (result status)
cases.rationale: An unknown status is never assumed final.

cases.failureMode: missing result status
cases.rationale: An absent status cannot be defaulted to final.

cases.failureMode: status value set applied without a resource type
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F7). The status value set was not bound to a resource type, so Observation.status and DiagnosticReport.status were conflated and `partial`/`appended` — DiagnosticReport statuses — were accepted as observation statuses.

cases.failureMode: status value set applied to the wrong resource type
cases.rationale: A DiagnosticReport-level profile is a separate contract with a different value set; it is refused rather than approximated.

cases.failureMode: local mapping is not an exact equivalence
cases.rationale: A wider/narrower/inexact mapping is representable so it stays visible, and it is never rounded up to `equivalent`.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (mapping not asserted)
cases.rationale: A mapping that exists but is not asserted by a named authority carries no weight.

cases.failureMode: code system version not asserted by the profile
cases.rationale: Code systems are pinned to exact versions; an unpinned or unasserted version is not interpretable.

cases.failureMode: unit mismatch
cases.rationale: An observation reporting a different unit than its mapping declares is never auto-converted.

cases.failureMode: specimen mismatch
cases.rationale: Specimen type changes expected values; a capillary and a venous specimen are not interchangeable.

cases.failureMode: stale result
cases.rationale: A result older than the site's staleness limit no longer describes the patient's current state.

cases.failureMode: unknown-value coercion (staleness policy)
cases.rationale: An unknown staleness policy must not be read as 'never stale'.

cases.failureMode: missing effective time
cases.rationale: Without an effective time the result cannot be age-banded, which is the axis pediatric intervals turn on.

cases.failureMode: missing issued time
cases.rationale: Issued time is what makes a later correction detectable.

cases.failureMode: absent profile section crashes instead of failing closed
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F9). An absent `resultStatusPolicy` threw a TypeError rather than failing closed.

cases.failureMode: expired profile
cases.rationale: Terminology profiles expire exactly as laboratory profiles do.

cases.failureMode: superseded without successor
cases.rationale: A dangling successor pointer breaks the mapping-authority chain.

cases.failureMode: synthetic profile edited to claim laboratory authority
cases.rationale: A synthetic_example profile is structurally forbidden from asserting authority, so it cannot be quietly promoted into an approved site profile by flipping one field.

cases.failureMode: synthetic profile edited to claim a bound signature
cases.rationale: Same containment on the attestation axis, so the synthetic fixture cannot satisfy the P2 attachment gate either.

cases.failureMode: profile reclassified as site_asserted while retaining its synthetic marker
cases.rationale: Reclassifying is not enough to launder a synthetic document: a site_asserted profile may not carry a syntheticDeclaration, so the contradiction is caught rather than resolved in favour of the more permissive reading.

cases.failureMode: authority asserted with every identity field still null
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F1). The prose said `asserted` requires every sibling to be non-null; nothing enforced it, so `authority.assertion: asserted` with a null institution, director, credential, statement, and date was structurally valid. This mutation is two of the four edits that promoted the shipped synthetic fixture to `{decision: applicable, blockers: []}`.

cases.failureMode: signature declared bound with no attachment reference
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F1). '`bound` additionally requires attachmentRef' was prose, not a constraint. It is now a conditional — and independently, the activation gate refuses a self-declared `bound` because no attachment verifier exists on this side.

cases.failureMode: container asserted while a secondary value is null
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F3). The `assertion` discriminator guarded only one key per container; every other value was a silent wildcard when null. It is now enforced across all siblings.

cases.failureMode: container asserted while a secondary value is null (altitude)
cases.rationale: Same defect on the altitude axis, where a null bound previously matched every altitude.

cases.failureMode: asserted age band with no upper bound
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 C3). An unbounded band is now structurally invalid, not merely refused at evaluation time.

cases.failureMode: asserted interval with a null sex
cases.rationale: Null is not a wildcard on an asserted interval; `any` must be written deliberately.

cases.failureMode: asserted critical value with neither a low nor a high threshold
cases.rationale: An alert that can never fire is indistinguishable from a configured one, which is the worst state for an urgent path.

cases.failureMode: attempt to introduce an 'assume default' assertion state
cases.rationale: The assertion enum admits exactly asserted / unknown / not_supplied. There is deliberately no state that means 'infer it'.

cases.failureMode: terminology profile opting out of status-lineage proof
cases.rationale: requireStatusLineage is a const, not a boolean: a profile that opts out cannot detect a dropped correction at all, so opting out is refused at the schema level.

cases.failureMode: terminology profile accepting preliminary results for decision use
cases.rationale: `preliminary` is excluded from the acceptedForDecision enum by construction, so no site can configure its way into treating preliminary results as final.

cases.failureMode: terminology profile accepting a DiagnosticReport status for Observation decision use
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F7). `appended` is a DiagnosticReport status and was permitted in acceptedForDecision, so a site could accept a status its observations can never legally carry.

cases.failureMode: terminology profile recognizing a DiagnosticReport status as an Observation status
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F7). `partial` is not a member of FHIR R4/R5 ObservationStatus. The value set is now bound to an explicit resourceType.

cases.failureMode: terminology profile omitting a required blocking state
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F7). 'Must include preliminary, unknown, entered-in-error, and cancelled' was prose and was unenforced, so a site could simply omit them.

cases.failureMode: asserted mapping with a null unit code
cases.rationale: REGRESSION (P3-V1 F3). A null mapping unit was truthiness-guarded into a wildcard at evaluation time; it is now invalid at the structural layer too.
